crossSite.context: FR season hub → EN season hub. Topical bridge: a Caribbean traveler reading about the MQ season is the exact audience for the US destinations. Place inside a contextual 'Voyage ailleurs aux Antilles ?' module at the foot of the season narrative (in-content, not footer), sculpting the strongest FR authority page toward the nascent EN season hub. dofollow.
anchorExamples: Sargasses en Floride : la saison côté Atlantique
Vous voyagez aux Antilles ? La saison des sargasses à Miami
Et de l'autre côté : la saison à Miami (Floride)

crossSite.context: FR season hub → EN season hub. Same Caribbean-network bridge from the GP authority page toward Punta Cana's season hub. Contextual module 'Voyage aux Antilles ?'
anchorExamples: La saison des sargasses à Punta Cana (Rép. dominicaine)
Vous partez en République dominicaine ? Suivez la saison à Punta Cana
Punta Cana : quand arrivent les sargasses

crossSite.context: FR season hub → ES season hub (Cancún). Caribbean bridge toward the Spanish-first nascent domain; useful for Francophone travelers heading to the Riviera Maya and signals topical relatedness FR→ES. Contextual module.
anchorExamples: La saison du sargazo à Cancún (Riviera Maya)
Cancún / Riviera Maya : la saison côté Caraïbe mexicaine
Vous filez au Mexique ? Le sargazo à Cancún

crossSite.context: FR homepage → EN homepage, same curated network module. GP pairs naturally with Punta Cana (close Caribbean travel corridor).

crossSite.context: EN season hub → EN season hub (Cancún's EN set via rivieramaya.en.json). Lateral EN authority sharing between season hubs across the Gulf/Caribbean; a Florida traveler comparing destinations is high-intent for Cancún.

Comparing the Gulf side: Cancún's sargassum season

crossSite.context: EN season hub → EN season hub. Closes the EN season triangle (Miami↔PuntaCana↔Cancún) so each nascent EN page has sibling inbound links and a shared topical cluster.

Comparing with Florida? Here's Miami's season

hubs.//: Map = primary hub. Links DOWN to every spoke from a persistent in-content hub-nav block; each beach spoke links back UP to /seaweed-map/ and to /beaches-without-sargassum-today/ via breadcrumb + 'See the live map' CTA.

Season = secondary hub for the safety+best+month cluster. Season → health-risks (when it's bad, is it dangerous?), → best beaches (where to go instead), → forecast/today (act now). Each spoke links back to season in its intro ('part of the Florida sargassum season guide').
Forecast hub → 7-day this-week page and per-beach forecasts; spokes link back up to /sargassum-forecast/.
Mirror of Miami. Resorts are extra spokes here (Punta Cana is resort-driven); each resort page links up to its parent beach and to the map.
Season secondary hub, same pattern as Miami.
ES-primary site. Map hub links down to all ES spokes; each playa links back up to /es/mapa-sargazo/ and /es/playas-sin-sargazo-hoy/.
ES season hub clusters best-beaches + this-week + forecast. Cancún also exposes an EN set (sargassum-season-cancun etc.) hreflang-paired but the ES set carries the intra-site hub authority.

FR flagship hub. Already strong; the job is to make sure each of the 136 beach spokes links UP to /carte-sargasses/ and to /previsions/ + /bulletin/ (breadcrumb + 'voir la carte' CTA), and that /carte-sargasses/ links DOWN to the regional hubs and a curated set of beaches (nearby/popular), not all 136 inline.
FR season hub mirrors the EN season-cluster pattern (season→best→this-week→forecast/bulletin), so the FR→EN season cross-links land on structurally identical pages (clean hreflang/topical parity).
GP flagship hub, same pattern as MQ.

rules: LINK BUDGET: contextual (in-content) internal links capped at ~8 per page; the shared cross-site network module adds at most 3 sibling-domain links. Never exceed ~12 internal+network links total in body copy (excludes nav/breadcrumb/footer chrome).

TWO LINK CLASSES, kept separate: (1) CONTEXTUAL links live inside narrative copy with descriptive anchors and pass topical authority; (2) NETWORK-MODULE links live in a single labelled block ('Voyage aux Antilles ?' / 'Traveling the Caribbean?' / '¿Viajas por el Caribe?') and are capped + rotated. Do not duplicate the same target in both classes on one page.

ANCHOR VARIETY: never reuse the identical anchor string for the same target across the site; maintain ≥3 anchor variants per cross-site target (see anchorExamples) and rotate deterministically by source page id so the link graph looks editorial, not templated. No naked-URL or 'click here' anchors.

BREADCRUMBS on every spoke (BreadcrumbList JSON-LD already emitted by region-seo-pages.cjs): Home → Hub → Spoke. Breadcrumb links count as the guaranteed upward inbound link for beach/resort/month/safety spokes.

CROSS-SITE = FR→EN/ES is the authority-sculpting direction (strong→nascent) and should be present on the highest-authority FR pages (homepage, /carte-sargasses/, /saison.../). Reverse links (EN/ES→FR) are allowed ONLY as reciprocal hreflang on true translations or sparingly in the network module — do not bleed nascent authority back to the strong domains.

HREFLANG only between TRUE translations of the same destination (e.g. Cancún EN ↔ Cancún ES). Cross-DESTINATION links (Miami→Cancún) are plain contextual/network links, NEVER hreflang alternates — mislabelling them as hreflang would confuse Google's clustering.

RELEVANCE GATE for the network module: a FR beach/hub may link a sibling region only via the curated module, and only to that sibling's TOP hub (today/map/season) — never deep-link a FR page straight to a single foreign beach (low relevance, spam signal).

DETERMINISTIC ROTATION on the 136 FR beach pages: each page surfaces exactly ONE network-module sibling link, chosen by hash(beachId) % 3 across {Miami today, PuntaCana today, Cancún hoy}, so authority is spread evenly and no single EN/ES hub is hammered by 136 identical links.

HONESTY DOCTRINE in anchors/snippets: cross-site teasers may state the product ('live satellite map, refreshed 4×/day', 'Beach Score 0–100', 'published backtest accuracy') but must NOT quote a fabricated global reliability %, fake review counts, or testimonials. Reliability is stated per-regime or omitted.

VOICE: network-module and bridge anchors stay in the 'Le Veilleur / The Watcher / El Vigía' register ('Le Veilleur veille aussi à Miami') — measured, never hypey. One consistent module label per language.

HUB→SPOKE reciprocity is mandatory: if a hub lists a spoke, that spoke MUST link back to the hub (breadcrumb satisfies it for beaches; hubs/cluster pages need an explicit in-copy 'part of the … guide' link). Enforce in build with a link-graph assertion (every generated spoke has ≥1 same-site upward link AND its hub references it).
orphanStrategy: "Guarantee ≥2 internal inbound links for every new/spoke page through three independent, build-enforced channels so no page depends on a single source. (1) BREADCRUMB inbound — every beach/resort/month/safety/best/this-week page is emitted with a BreadcrumbList whose Hub node is a real link (Home→Hub→page); this is the first guaranteed inbound from the cluster hub. (2) HUB-NAV block — each site's primary hub (/seaweed-map/, /carte-sargasses/, /es/mapa-sargazo/) renders a hub-nav listing every sibling hub + the season/safety/best/this-week pages, giving each of those a second guaranteed inbound; and each season hub lists its best/this-week/safety spokes (third inbound for those). (3) RELATED-IN-CLUSTER — beach/resort pages link to 2–3 nearby beaches/resorts in the same region (by geo proximity), and 'today'/'best' pages enumerate the top-N beaches by score, so deep pages collect multiple sibling-to-sibling inbounds beyond the breadcrumb. Cross-site network-module links are ADDITIVE, never counted toward the ≥2 minimum (a foreign-domain link doesn't cure an intrasite orphan). BUILD GUARD: add an assertion to region-seo-pages.cjs / vite.config.js that crawls the generated link graph and FAILS the build if any emitted page has <2 same-site inbound links, listing the offenders — the same guard that enforces hub↔spoke reciprocity. New drafted pages (safety, season variants) are wired into both the hub-nav and the season-cluster lists at generation time, so they are never shipped orphaned."
